name: Profile_R5_DeviceDispense_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.DeviceDispense resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 DeviceDispense resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.DeviceDispense resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 DeviceDispense resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: R5: The order or request that this dispense is fulfilling (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.basedOn` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.basedOn` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The bigger event that this dispense is a part of (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.partOf` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.partOf` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Why a dispense was or was not performed (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.statusReason` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.statusReason` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Type of device dispense (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.category` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.category` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The category can be used to include where the device is expected to be consumed or other types of dispenses.  Invariants can be used to bind to different value sets when profiling to bind.

element.short: R5: Full representation of the usage instructions (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.usageInstruction` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.usageInstruction` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The content of the renderedDosageInstructions must not be different than the dose represented in the dosageInstruction content.

element.short: R5: A list of relevant lifecycle events (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.eventHistory` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.eventHistory` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This might not include provenances for all versions of the request – only those deemed “relevant” or important. This SHALL NOT include the Provenance associated with this current version of the resource. (If that provenance is deemed to be a “relevant” change, it will need to be added as part of a later update. Until then, it can be queried directly as the Provenance that points to this version using _revinclude All Provenances should have some historical version of this Request as their subject.).

element.short: R5: preparation | in-progress | cancelled | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | declined | unknown (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This element is labeled as a modifier because the status contains codes that mark the resource as not currently valid.
